name: legal-toolkit
description: In-house legal toolkit for 台灣 SME → 上市櫃 法務 — 7-layer schema-driven contract review (Stark / Adams / Burnham + Taiwan overlay: 強行/任意 規定二分 / 定型化契約 §247-1 / 六準則解釋), playbook-driven negotiation (visible per-clause Markdown + variant-folder schema, ABAC pre-filter, walk-away / fallback / preferred 三層 escalation), and disclaimer-driven outputs (Mandatory Disclaimer + Escalation Override on high-risk findings). 3 skills: using-legal-toolkit (router) + legal-playbook-author (bootstrap/extend/revise) + legal-contract-review (review/redline/nda mode). v0.2.0 (Phase 1.5) expanded bundled fallback from 4 to 8 clauses (variant-folder limitation-of-liability / indemnification / data-protection-dpa keyed by deal_size or jurisdiction + flat ip-assignment-and-license) and added Python scripts (discover_playbook / validate_schema / detect_conflicts / abac_filter / build_baseline / seed_baseline) plus JSON Schema 2020-12 frontmatter validation. v0.3.0 (Phase 1.6) adds the eval-tier rubric (20-criterion answer + 10-criterion source + 12-criterion playbook-quality, each tagged deterministic vs semantic) and self_grade.py that runs the 22 deterministic checks against contract-review output (writes self-grade.md), plus a calibration procedure for hand-grading 5-10 contracts against Pearson targets ≥ 0.6 / ≥ 0.7. v0.3.1 dogfood-driven patches: L7 Step 1.5 stance-asymmetry pass (favorable-position absences no longer flagged as risk); L6 sub-check 4 vagueness scan (catches 'clause present but sub-mechanism vague'); L7 Step 9.3 citation applicability gate; SRC-04 + statute-articles.json blacklist closes the SRC-09 escape via fabricated sub-articles + deprecated §27; banner placement narrowed to memo-legal + escalation only; NDA mode layer dispatch aligned across SKILL.md + L7 + self_grade.py; findings.json schema documented. English skill instructions + zh-TW domain content + bilingual triggers. Phase 2-5 adds document-draft / incident-response / issue-spot / research / contract-tracker / regulation-watch / corporate-governance / dd-quickscan — see ROADMAP.md.
